datasets.description: TEP_KG RCA graph built from materials (Simulink, MATLAB, C, prior graph, ontology). Includes FaultAnchor and SemanticConcept nodes.
datasets.label: TEP RCA Graph

policies: RCA Graph inherits the semantic lift backbone and adds SemanticConcept nodes without mutating the original Full KG entities.
REALIZES links evidence or implementation nodes to semantic concepts, while PART_OF anchors concepts onto RCA-relevant entities.
FaultAnchor nodes model canonical root-cause sources and connect to process entities only through propagating FAULT_SOURCE edges.
Curated propagation bridges restore downstream analyzer reachability where the prior graph omits outlet-stream-to-component carriers.
Live LLM curation is optional; rule-based candidate groups remain the deterministic fallback path.

attributes.summary: Monitors component concentrations in product and purge streams using analyzers to track production quality and purge composition.
Manages reactor level, pressure, and cooling via agitator and cooling coil, with feed from multiple streams.
Handles recycle compression and purge rate control, with analysis of purge composition to manage inert buildup.
Controls feed rates of A, C, D, E and mixed ABC feed, with feedforward and analyzer monitoring.
Operates stripper with steam supply, managing flow, level, and overhead/bottom product separation.
Manages condensation and vapor-liquid separation, controlling coolant and separator conditions.
Canonical root-cause anchor for A/C-ratio deviations in stream 4.
Canonical root-cause anchor for B-composition deviations in stream 4.
Canonical root-cause anchor for stream-2 D-feed temperature deviations.
Canonical root-cause anchor for reactor cooling-water inlet temperature faults.
Canonical root-cause anchor for separator cooling-water inlet temperature faults.
Canonical root-cause anchor for A-feed loss on stream 1.
Canonical root-cause anchor for C-header pressure loss on stream 4.
Canonical root-cause anchor for random A/B/C composition deviations in stream 4.
Canonical root-cause anchor for stream-4 feed temperature deviations.
Canonical root-cause anchor for reaction-kinetics drift inside the reactor.
Canonical root-cause anchor for reactor coolant-valve stiction faults.
Canonical root-cause anchor for separator coolant-valve stiction faults.
Canonical root-cause anchor for stripper-side heat-transfer deviations.
Canonical root-cause anchor for reactor-side heat-transfer deviations.
Canonical root-cause anchor for condenser and separator heat-transfer deviations.
Canonical umbrella anchor for the ambiguous multi-valve stiction disturbance family.

datasets.description: KGTraceVis task-oriented knowledge graph covering MVTec product defects, TEP timeseries, and Wafer fab-log scenarios. Source-constrained with confidence assignments and review status.

attributes.evidence: Bottle example can contain scratch defect evidence
Scratch captions and elongated masks support linear morphology
Scratch is treated as a surface defect in the visual RCA reference layer
XMEAS_1 is used as a reactor-related example variable
Abnormal XMEAS_1 can indicate the example process fault
The example process fault is associated with the reactor unit
The example process fault may be explained by a feed flow disturbance
Wafer example contains nearfull defect evidence
Nearfull defects are represented with dense particle morphology in the example KG
Nearfull defect affects the wafer surface in the example case
Example alarm is associated with the nearfull wafer case
Insufficient glue removal may cause nearfull-like dense contamination
Metal nut scratch is selected as a development RCA case because linear surface marks are visually consistent with contact or handling damage
Scratch is a linear surface defect consistent with contact damage
Mechanical contact is grouped under handling-related damage
Repeated surface abrasion can produce shallow scratch-like marks
Localized color change can indicate residue staining or foreign material on the surface
Color variation can also reflect material or coating inconsistency
Cable cut defects are selected as development RCA cases for mechanical or assembly-related damage
Cut-like cable damage is visually consistent with sharp contact or cutting damage
Missing wire evidence maps to a missing component root-cause category
Missing wire evidence can be explained by an assembly omission or placement error
Bent wire evidence can indicate assembly or routing error
Bent wire evidence can also indicate fixture or positioning misalignment
Cracks can indicate brittle material or formation defects
Cracks can also be consistent with compression during packaging or transport
Visible contamination maps to a foreign material or residue source
Squeeze-like deformation is consistent with compression or packaging pressure
Squeeze-like deformation can also be caused by handling damage

datasets.label: MVTec Runtime KG

nodes.description: Linear surface defect used in the visual RCA example
nodes.name: Scratch defect

nodes.description: Plausible damage during handling or transport
nodes.name: Handling damage

nodes.description: Plausible fixture or process alignment cause
nodes.name: Process misalignment

nodes.description: Plausible process root cause for example fault
nodes.name: Feed flow disturbance
